name: Castellar del Vallès and Sabadell Loop
name_original: Can Gambús y Castellar del Vallés – Circular desde el sur de Sabadell
description: This moderate 27.7-mile road cycling route from Sabadell offers picturesque rural landscapes and passes through Castellar del Vallès.
region_intro: Road cycling around Sabadell offers diverse terrain, from flat stretches to challenging ascents, catering to various skill levels. The region is characterized by its proximity to natural parks like Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac and Montseny, providing varied landscapes for cyclists. The Ripoll River Park also offers a dedicated cycling route along its course. This varied topography includes both demanding climbs and quieter rural roads, making it suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Sabadell The most popular road cycling route is Castellar del Vallès and Sabadell Loop, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced ride through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castellar del Vallès – Sant Llorenç Savall loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord, a Difficult 63.3 miles (101.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, challenging experienced riders with its demanding ascents. Local road cyclists also love the Sant Llorenç Savall – Castellar del Vallès loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail leading through the scenic areas around Sant Llorenç Savall and Castellar del Vallès, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Sabadell is defined by its access to natural parks, river paths, and varied topography, including both flat sections and demanding climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult long-distance routes. The routes in Sabadell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sabadell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sabadell?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Sabadell, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 8 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Sabadell?
A: The region around Sabadell offers a varied topography, from flat stretches ideal for speed training to demanding ascents. You'll find routes traversing natural parks like Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac and Montseny, as well as quieter rural roads and dedicated paths along the Ripoll River Park.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sabadell?
A: Yes, Sabadell offers 8 easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for a comfortable introduction to road cycling in the area.

Q: What about challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Experienced road cyclists will find 12 difficult routes around Sabadell, featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31290071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar del Vallès – Sant Llorenç Savall loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 100 km with more than 1,500 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Sabadell?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Sabadell are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995629293/castellar-del-valles-and-sabadell-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar del Vallès and Sabadell Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Llorenç Savall – Castellar del Vallès loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic rides through rural environments.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling around Sabadell?
A: You can explore diverse natural landscapes, including the rugged terrain and distinctive monoliths of Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park, the rich biodiversity of Montseny Natural Park, and the peaceful green areas along the Ripoll River Park. The Rodal de Sabadell also offers picturesque rural scenery connecting urban areas with the countryside.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by historical landmarks. You can encounter various castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/649491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentmenat Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanta Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola and the Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt&lt;/a&gt;, offering historical insights and stunning views.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on road cycling routes near Sabadell?
A: For breathtaking views, consider routes that ascend to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola&lt;/a&gt; in Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park, which provides expansive panoramic views of Catalonia. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6094845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat del Vent&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1426429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallvidrera Pass - Tibidabo&lt;/a&gt;, both offering spectacular vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sabadell?
A: Sabadell is suitable for road cycling year-round due to its varied topography. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides. Summer can be warm, but early morning or late afternoon rides are enjoyable. Even in winter, many routes remain accessible, especially on sunny days.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants. The region is known for cyclist-friendly amenities, with establishments like Frankfurt Castellar offering breakfast options and facilities for cyclists. It's always a good idea to carry water, but refills are often available in villages.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Sabadell?
A: The road cycling routes around Sabadell are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural landscapes of the surrounding parks, and the well-maintained network of routes that cater to all ability levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Sabadell?
A: Yes, Sabadell serves as an excellent starting point for long-distance road cycling. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31290071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar del Vallès – Sant Llorenç Savall loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord&lt;/a&gt; extend over 100 km, providing a significant challenge for those looking to cover longer distances and experience extensive elevation gains.
tips: Quiet road between a golf course that avoids the busiest roads from Terrasa. Perfect way to go up to the Obac.
Town in the Vallés Occidental region that is located at the midpoint between Sabadell and Tarrasa, but despite this it maintains a very quiet and country atmosphere.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular road cycling route is designed to be completed in approximately 2 hours and 5 minutes, based on an average cycling pace. Your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it easy to ride for road bikes. While it includes some elevation gain, it's generally an undulating terrain rather than consistently steep climbs, offering a smooth cycling experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a pleasant blend of natural and rural landscapes. A notable segment is the quiet Golf Road, which helps avoid busier areas. You'll also pass through Castellar del Vallès, a town known for its calm and rural atmosphere, offering picturesque countryside views.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as 'moderate' and generally requires a good fitness level. While the surfaces are well-paved, the distance and elevation gain suggest it's best suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the pleasant rural atmosphere and well-paved roads, spring and autumn are generally ideal for cycling this route, offering comfortable temperatures. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though it might be warmer.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or highlights?
faqs.answer: Yes, key highlights include the peaceful Golf Road and the charming town of Castellar del Vallès, which provides a tranquil, rural experience. You'll also ride along Can Valls Road.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several official trails, including sections of El Camí IT3TR2 El Camí Metropolità: de Montserrat a Barcelona, Sender del Vallès Natural, and TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània, among others.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a public drinking fountain along the route, which is a convenient amenity for cyclists to refill water bottles.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from the south of Sabadell. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find street parking in residential areas near the starting point or look for public parking facilities within Sabadell before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling path. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not specifically designed as a dog-friendly trail. If you bring a dog, ensure it's well-behaved, on a leash, and consider if the road environment is suitable for your pet.
